Mia winds down after a full, energetic day and gets a solid night's sleep; the overnight beat spans from late evening to early morning as required.

diary: Woke up early with coffee and my dog—she seemed to know something good was coming. Got to the studio in Capitol Hill before the crowds hit, threw some pieces that actually felt right for once. The clay was cooperating, which meant I could slip out early and catch the match with friends at The Crocodile. USA vs Wales had everyone wound up, and I grabbed lunch between plays. The energy downtown was infectious, even if the light rail was down and everyone was rerouted. Swung by Whole Foods after, picked up some wild mushrooms and fresh herbs for something special. Made a leisurely lunch at home, then felt too restless to stay put, so I drove out to Discovery Park as the afternoon mellowed. The walk cleared my head. Back home now with the dog, dinner settling in my stomach, and the roof all set up for stargazing. It's one of those Mondays that feels like a gift—productive morning, good friends, good food, and now the sky opening up above me.
thought_process: I was energized this morning and wanted to maximize the studio time before the match disrupted everything. The World Cup energy downtown was contagious, and honestly, I needed the social break—ceramics can be isolating. Skipping the light rail was fine since I was already in Capitol Hill; the transit closure barely touched my day. After lunch, I could've gone back to work, but the weather was too perfect and my mood was too restless. Discovery Park felt like the right move—something about being outside grounds me. By evening, I just wanted quiet rituals: cooking, my dog, the stars. The day felt balanced without being forced, which doesn't happen often.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
